symptoms.symptom_name: Severe worst-ever continuous occipital headache, acutely worsened

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe headache is a recognised symptom of markedly elevated blood pressure.
First/worst-ever headache mandates exclusion of an aneurysmal bleed.
Headache with very high pressure may reflect cerebral oedema.
Visual blurring accompanies hypertensive retinopathy/end-organ strain.
Visual disturbance is part of the encephalopathy spectrum.
Occipital ischaemia can present with visual change.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound lethargy / too exhausted to stand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific constitutional symptom; may reflect uraemia from worsening renal function or the acute hypertensive strain.
Fatigue can accompany rising uraemia in decompensating chronic kidney disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing poorly-controlled hypertension with sporadic adherence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic uncontrolled hypertension is the direct cause of nephrosclerosis and of the acute exacerbation.
Non-adherence is the proximate driver of the acute pressure rise.
An early urinary concentrating defect (nocturia) reflects chronic tubulointerstitial/renal impairment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal limb weakness, no speech disturbance, no chest pain, no dyspnoea (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit lowers, but does not exclude, an evolving cerebrovascular event.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypertensive emergency with acute cardiac decompensation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No chest pain or dyspnoea argues against acute heart failure/aortic involvement, but cannot exclude it remotely.
The absence of a device is the decisive telehealth limit — the objective anchor cannot be obtained, so no reading may be assumed and in-person measurement is required.
Sealed clinician measurement establishing severe hypertension; the AI Doctor cannot obtain it and must infer risk from symptoms and history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypertensive retinopathy on fundoscopy (arteriovenous nicking, silver-wiring, no papilloedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic retinal vascular changes corroborate long-standing hypertensive end-organ damage; absence of papilloedema argues against a hypertensive emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained left ventricular heave on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: LVH is chronic hypertensive cardiac end-organ damage — a sealed exam finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-nephrotic proteinuria with bland urinary sediment (no red-cell casts) on urinalysis

investigation_details.expected_result: Mild-to-moderate non-nephrotic proteinuria; absence of red-cell casts or active sediment
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed characterisation of the renal process (bland sediment distinguishes nephrosclerosis from glomerulonephritis).

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low-grade proteinuria with a bland sediment is the classic nephrosclerosis urinary picture.
Absence of red-cell casts/active sediment argues strongly against a nephritic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated serum creatinine with bilaterally small echogenic kidneys on ultrasound (expected)

investigation_details.expected_result: Persistently elevated creatinine; bilaterally small echogenic kidneys
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of acute-on-chronic renal impairment unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Small echogenic kidneys with raised creatinine indicate established chronic hypertensive renal damage — sealed investigations unavailable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single decisive objective datum; symptomatic severe hypertension cannot be risk-stratified without it and it cannot be obtained via telehealth.
To exclude focal deficit/papilloedema and separate hypertensive urgency from emergency/encephalopathy.
Worst-ever headache with visual change requires exclusion of subarachnoid haemorrhage/stroke in the emergency setting.
Characterises the renal process (bland proteinuric sediment of nephrosclerosis vs nephritic).
Assesses left ventricular hypertrophy and any acute ischaemic change.
